scope.target: Retain the repository pattern baseline analysis and create a decision-safe remediation workflow
scope.dependency_radius: Repository standards, architecture references, prompts, guides, examples, and transitional validation scripts
constraints: Do not modify canonical repository standards before user decisions are recorded
Do not treat historical examples as current product truth
Do not use the current repository regex scripts as a compliance gate
Do not create commits without user authorization

inputs.architecture_target: Create one coherent DDD, Clean Architecture, CQRS, and Hexagonal repository pattern contract
review_findings: Command-side persistence conflicts between Dapper/Npgsql and EF Core
Repository abstraction name and method count are inconsistent
Soft-delete, Unit of Work, outbox, and event-clear timing are not coherent
Query-side layering and custom-interface placement conflict
Generated repository checks do not implement the current standard
user_constraints: List decisions required from the user before implementation

steps: Applied the workflow gate and selected workflow mode
Read the repository architecture, tech-stack, repository, query, workflow, and context-governance sources
Retained evidence-backed findings in a review report
Defined staged remediation, validation, and commit checkpoints
Recorded decisions that must not be inferred
validation: Parse all workflow task JSON files
Confirm the workflow is awaiting user decisions

results.summary: Opened the workflow, retained the evidence-backed repository pattern analysis, defined staged remediation, and recorded nine decisions that must be resolved before canonical standards change.
tests_run: Parsed all workflow task JSON files with PowerShell ConvertFrom-Json
Validated local Markdown links in workflow-plan.md and review-report.md
